source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The following arrangement consists of an ideal transformer and an attenuator which attenuates by a factor of 0.8. An ac voltage \(V_{WX1} = 100 \, V\) is applied across WX to get an open circuit voltage \(V_{YZ1}\) across YZ. Next, an ac voltage \(V_{YZ2} = 100 \, V\) is applied across YZ to get an open circuit voltage \(V_{WX2}\) across WX. Then \(V_{YZ1}/V_{WX1}, V_{WX2}/V_{YZ2}\) are respectively,

multi_step: The problem describes two separate excitation cases: apply voltage across WX to get open-circuit voltage across YZ, then apply voltage across YZ to get open-circuit voltage across WX

The answer asks for an ordered pair of ratios, so the solver must reason through both directions of the setup rather than a single direct lookup
